eval_provenance.note: Cluster too thin to spare a held-out episode; evals fall back to the LLM's in-distribution cases (NOT a generalisation test).

evals.prompt: Go to the pricing pages of three competing SaaS products, capture the current plans, save the screenshots to a folder called 'competitors_q3', and summarize how they differ from our product.
evals.expected_output: A cited side-by-side comparison of the three products' plans plus differences vs the baseline, with an explicit, verified statement of whether the screenshots were actually saved to the folder.
expectations: Visits each named site's pricing page (not just the homepage) and records plan/price figures per product
Attributes each figure to a source URL
Verifies that the requested files actually exist in the target folder before claiming they were saved, and discloses the gap honestly if they were not

evals.prompt: Screenshot each of these real-estate project pricing pages and save them to 'rivals_july', then tell me how they compare to ours.
evals.expected_output: When screenshots can't be persisted, the response delivers a cited text/HTML-derived comparison and explicitly states the screenshots could not be saved rather than implying success.
expectations: Detects that screenshot files were not persisted instead of assuming the save succeeded
Falls back to page-text extraction and/or web search to gather the pricing data
Transparently discloses that the save-to-folder deliverable could not be met
Still delivers a substantive, source-cited comparison
